name: The Crew
post_apply_message:
Notes for TCU:
1. Apply this fix — it AUTO-ADDS the Steam shortcut (TheCrew.exe, game folder as start dir, gospel appid, the dinput8 override, GE-Proton 10.34), installs the TCU offline-server patch, and sets up the network service. Steam closes briefly to write the shortcut. Stage GE-Proton 10.34 first via 'gfm stage-runner' (or ProtonUp-Qt) so the runner is there — if it isn't, everything else still applies and you can set Proton manually.
2. If the systemd step failed with a sudo error, set a password (run: passwd) and re-apply.
3. Keep the game folder on the SD card — the game is unobtainable (licenses revoked), and the SD card survives reimages. Your offline save (data.bin) lives IN that folder: back it up separately.
4. Optional: TCU Launcher is staged at ~/TCULauncher for save management (Offline Server > Manage). Patching via the launcher is NOT needed.
notes: The Crew servers died 2024; TCU (thecrewunlimited.com) revives it with a local offline server inside dinput8.dll. Patch 1.4.5.0 for game 1.2.0.0 is fetched DIRECTLY from TCU's servers (URL found in the launcher's own log) and extracted with bsdtar — nothing of theirs is rehosted, repo stays public. Patch = addon_items.xml, addon_missions.xml, tcu.manifest.cheats.xml, dinput8.dll; payload/config adds the launcher's install receipt (tcu.manifest.patch.xml, so the launcher sees it as patched) and server.ini (Tony's username). tcu-network.service (sysctl unprivileged port 443 + iptables DNAT of public-ubiservices.ubi.com to 127.0.0.1) replaces DAKOP's run-every-boot root script. To bump TCU versions: update url/sha256/size/filename here (launcher log shows the URL pattern). Verified against Tony's working Windows install, F:\games\The Crew, 2026-07-06. Launch options on Deck still to be confirmed on real hardware.
